_meta.title: Execution model, runtime bindings & restart recovery
sourceDocs: Janumi Professional Workbench Recursive Professional Harness - Canonical Domain Model, Invariant Catalog, State Machines, and Event Contract
Janumi Professional Workbench Recursive Professional Harness - Executable Invariant and Conformance Test Specification
Janumi Professional Workbench Recursive Professional Harness - Command, Event, Schema Contract Package
Janumi Professional Workbench Recursive Professional Harness - Persistence, Migration, Dual-Run, and Cutover Design
_meta.purpose: Authoritative vocabulary for the RPH Execution Aggregate: ExecutionPlan lifecycle + approval, ExecutionStep lifecycle with pre/postconditions, RuntimeBinding capability authorization, the retry cap and exhaustion behavior, the execution-not-equals-assurance boundary (INV-5 / §35.2 at the execution layer), and restart/recovery + idempotency for in-flight execution.
_meta.sourceRefConvention: Bare '§N' = section number in the Canonical Domain Model doc (authoritative for object structure and enum spellings). 'Contract §N' = Command/Event/Schema Contract Package doc (operative for wire shapes and payloads). 'Persistence §N' = Persistence, Migration, Dual-Run, and Cutover Design doc (tables, outbox, recovery). Test ids (RPH-EXE-*, RPH-PWU-*, RPH-PER-*, RPH-E2E-*, RPH-FIX-*, Property P*) live in the Conformance Test Spec doc.
_meta.authoritativeNote: The Canonical Domain Model is authoritative for enum spellings and object structure. The Contract Package refines two shapes: ExecutionPlan embeds steps/transitions inline (steps: ExecutionStep[], transitions: ExecutionTransition[]) whereas Canonical §20.1 references them by id (stepIds, transitionIds); the Persistence schema normalizes them into separate tables with FKs, matching the Canonical id-reference model. The exec!=assurance boundary is stated verbatim in Contract §35.2 and Contract §16.2, mirrored by Canonical §35.1 (Property 'No execution implies assurance') and Conformance Property P1 + RPH-PWU-005. Divergences captured in _openItems.

executionPlan.description: An ExecutionPlan (objectType 'EXECUTION_PLAN', §4 ProfessionalWorkObjectType) is the aggregate root of the Execution Aggregate (§3.3), which owns Execution Plan, Execution Step, Execution Attempt, Execution Event, runtime result, and retry/tactical-change state. It describes HOW a PWU is currently intended to be performed (§2.3). A plan is a replaceable strategy: changing the plan does not change the identity or meaning of the PWU, and a PWU may have no plan, exactly one active plan, several superseded plans, or alternative candidate plans (§2.3). A plan may not change PWU intent or obligations, and plan approval grants NO runtime privileges (§20.2).
executionPlan.extends: ObjectEnvelope (§4: id, objectType, semanticVersion, revision, lifecycleStatus, provenance, authorityId, etc.)

fields.name: objectType
fields.description: Discriminant fixing this envelope as an ExecutionPlan.

fields.name: workUnitId
fields.description: The single PWU this plan performs. An active plan must reference exactly one PWU.

fields.name: planVersion
fields.description: Monotonic plan revision. Plan revision preserves prior attempt history. Unique per (work_unit_id, plan_version).

fields.name: stepIds
fields.description: Canonical §20.1 references steps by id. Contract §15 instead embeds them as 'steps: ExecutionStep[]'. Persistence normalizes into the execution_steps table (FK execution_plan_id). See _openItems.

fields.name: transitionIds
fields.description: Canonical §20.1 references transitions by id; Contract §15 embeds 'transitions: ExecutionTransition[]'; Persistence normalizes into execution_transitions (source_step_id, target_step_id, condition_expression, transition_type).

fields.name: retryPolicy
fields.description: Policy governing retry count/backoff for step attempts. Shape not defined in the docs (see _openItems); RPH-EXE-008 exercises a policy whose maximum is three attempts.

fields.name: tacticalChangePolicy
fields.description: Authorizes in-plan tactical changes. Tactical changes may occur within a plan only if authorized by policy.

fields.name: escalationPolicy
fields.description: Governs escalation when execution cannot proceed.

fields.name: terminationPolicy
fields.description: Governs plan/step termination conditions.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Lifecycle state of the plan itself.

statuses.meaning: Plan proposed (proposeExecutionPlan / ExecutionPlanProposed); not yet approved or active.

Plan approved (approveExecutionPlan / ExecutionPlanApproved). Approval is required before irreversible execution for high-risk work, and PWU READY->PLANNED requires an active execution plan approved. Approval grants no runtime privileges.

Plan activated (activateExecutionPlan / ExecutionPlanActivated) and driving execution. A PWU may have only one active plan at a time (enforced by uq_active_execution_plan_per_pwu WHERE status='ACTIVE').
All required steps reached terminal success; plan finished.
Plan terminated in failure per failure taxonomy §36.2 (e.g., retry exhaustion, dependency unavailable).
Replaced by a successor plan (ExecutionPlanSuperseded). A superseded plan cannot create new execution attempts and no new step may begin under it.

stateMachine.note: No dedicated ExecutionPlan transition table exists in the docs; the machine is the status enum (§20.1) + execution commands (§34.3) + execution events (§26.4) + the PWU lifecycle rows that gate on plan state (§8.1).

transitions.guard: Approval required before irreversible execution for high-risk work (§20.2). Gates PWU READY->PLANNED (§8.1).

Only one ACTIVE plan per PWU (RPH-EXE-001); ActivateExecutionPlanPayload carries approvalDecisionId? and authorizedRuntimeBindingIds. Gates PWU PLANNED->EXECUTING which requires 'Runtime bindings authorized' (§8.1).
A new plan supersedes; superseded plan cannot start steps (RPH-EXE-002) or create new attempts (§20.2).

executionPlan.approvalRule: Plan approval is required before irreversible execution for high-risk work (§20.2). Approval is a distinct governance gate: the PWU transition READY -> PLANNED requires 'Active execution plan approved' (§8.1), and the subsequent PLANNED -> EXECUTING requires 'Runtime bindings authorized' (§8.1). Plan approval does NOT grant runtime privileges (§20.2) -- capability authorization is a separate RuntimeBinding step. Activation carries an optional approvalDecisionId plus the set of authorizedRuntimeBindingIds (Contract §15.2). RPH-EXE-001 enforces exactly one ACTIVE plan per PWU; RPH-EXE-002 blocks step start under a SUPERSEDED plan; RPH-PWU-010 blocks resuming execution in place against a BASELINED PWU semantic version (a successor revision/PWU is required).

invariants.statement: An active plan must reference exactly one PWU.
A PWU may have only one active plan at a time.
A plan may not change PWU intent or obligations.
Plan approval is required before irreversible execution for high-risk work.
Runtime privileges are not granted by plan approval.
A superseded plan cannot create new execution attempts.
Plan revision preserves prior attempt history.
Tactical changes may occur within a plan only if authorized by policy.

rules.statement: One active plan per PWU: activating a second plan without superseding the first is rejected.
Superseded plan cannot start step: a step-start request against a plan in SUPERSEDED is rejected.
Baselined PWU cannot resume execution in place: new execution against the same semantic version of a BASELINED PWU is rejected; a successor revision or successor PWU is required.

executionStep.description: An ExecutionStep is a single unit of the plan's execution graph, owned by the Execution Aggregate. It carries a stepType, input/output bindings, an optional RuntimeBinding, explicit pre/postconditions, and a stepState. A step cannot run until its preconditions are satisfied; a succeeded step must record outputs (or an explicit no-output result); every tool/model invocation must produce provenance; and external outputs are untrusted until parsed and validated. Step success does NOT imply PWU success.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Step identifier.

fields.name: executionPlanId
fields.description: Owning plan (FK).

fields.name: stepType
fields.description: Kind of execution action.

fields.name: purpose
fields.description: What the step is for.

fields.name: inputBindings
fields.description: Inputs consumed. External inputs are untrusted until parsed and validated.

fields.name: outputBindings
fields.description: Outputs produced. A succeeded step must record outputs or an explicit no-output result.

fields.name: runtimeBindingId
fields.description: The RuntimeBinding that grants the step its runtime (agent/model/tool/sandbox/capabilities). Must be AUTHORIZED before the step executes.

fields.name: preconditions
fields.description: Must all be satisfied before the step may run.

fields.name: postconditions
fields.description: Must hold on step success; success requires an explicit result.

fields.name: stepState
fields.description: Lifecycle state of the step.

fields.name: ordinal
fields.description: Ordering position (persistence only; not in the Canonical interface).

stepTypes.meaning: Group of steps executed in parallel.
Invoke an assurance evaluation (a step may TRIGGER assurance, but its own success still does not set AssuranceState=SATISFIED).

stepStates.meaning: Preconditions not yet satisfied; may not run. RPH-EXE-005: a step whose required input artifact is absent remains not ready and no model/tool invocation occurs.
Preconditions satisfied; eligible to be scheduled (ExecutionStepReady).
Scheduled/queued for execution. (No dedicated ExecutionStepQueued event in §26.4 -- see _openItems.)
Completed successfully with recorded output/explicit no-output result (ExecutionStepSucceeded; resultingExecutionState 'SUCCEEDED'). Does NOT imply PWU success or AssuranceState=SATISFIED.
Attempt failed (ExecutionStepFailed); may retry within the RetryPolicy cap.
Skipped (ExecutionStepSkipped). A skipped MANDATORY step requires an authorized plan revision or waiver.
Superseded by a plan revision; cannot run. (No dedicated ExecutionStepSuperseded event in §26.4 -- see _openItems.)

executionStep.preconditionPostconditionRule: A step cannot run until ALL preconditions are satisfied (§21.1); if a required input artifact is absent the step remains NOT_READY and no model/tool invocation occurs (RPH-EXE-005). On completion, a succeeded step MUST record outputs or an explicit no-output result -- completion requested without output or an explicit no-output result is rejected (RPH-EXE-006, §21.1). Preconditions and postconditions are persisted as explicit jsonb (execution_steps.preconditions / .postconditions); state is explicit and never inferred from absent output (§2.6).

invariants.statement: A step cannot run until preconditions are satisfied.
A succeeded step must record outputs or an explicit no-output result.
Step success does not imply PWU success.
A skipped mandatory step requires an authorized plan revision or waiver.
Every tool or model invocation must produce provenance.
External outputs are untrusted until parsed and validated.

rules.statement: Step precondition enforcement: a step whose required input artifact is absent, when started, remains not ready and no model/tool invocation occurs.
Step success requires explicit result: completing a running step without output or an explicit no-output result is rejected.

Model output treated as untrusted: a malformed structured result retains raw output for diagnostics, fails boundary validation, does not create authoritative objects, and may trigger retry/alternate strategy.

executionAttempt.note: Execution Attempt is owned by the Execution Aggregate (§3.3) and is where retries and external side effects materialize; the Canonical §21 interface does not declare it but persistence does.

fields.name: attemptNumber
fields.note: Unique per (execution_step_id, attempt_number). The retry cap bounds this.

fields.name: idempotencyKey
fields.note: Unique (uq_execution_idempotency) -- prevents duplicate external side effects on retry.

fields.name: externalOperationId
fields.note: Observable external ID used for restart reconciliation.

fields.name: reconciliationState
fields.note: Set on restart when completion is uncertain; free-text (no enumerated values in docs).

runtimeBinding.description: A RuntimeBinding (objectType 'RUNTIME_BINDING') connects an ExecutionStep to its runtime: agents, models, tools, sandboxes, permissions, context policies, and observability policies (§2.4). It grants NO semantic authority -- it cannot redefine the intent or obligations of the work (§2.4). Requested capability is not granted capability; capability scope must be explicit; secret access must never be inferred from tool availability; privilege expansion requires a new authorization event; revoked bindings cannot be used for new attempts; and model output is treated as untrusted external input.

fields.name: objectType
fields.description: Discriminant.

fields.name: executionStepId
fields.description: The step this binding serves (FK).

fields.name: roleId
fields.description: The role/agent identity bound.

fields.name: modelSelectionPolicy
fields.description: Policy for selecting the model.

fields.name: requestedCapabilities
fields.description: Capabilities requested. Requested is NOT granted.

fields.name: grantedCapabilities
fields.description: Capabilities actually granted with explicit scope. Operations outside the grant fail authorization.

fields.name: sandboxPolicy
fields.description: Sandbox restrictions enforced by the Runtime Authorization Service.

fields.name: contextAssemblyPolicyId
fields.description: Context assembly policy reference.

fields.name: observabilityPolicyId
fields.description: Observability policy reference.

fields.name: memoryPolicyId
fields.description: Optional memory policy reference.

fields.name: authorizationStatus
fields.description: Authorization state of the binding.

authorizationStatuses.meaning: Capabilities requested but not yet authorized (RuntimeBindingRequested). Execution cannot start against a REQUESTED binding.
Fully authorized (RuntimeBindingAuthorized). Required before a step executes (PWU PLANNED->EXECUTING gate).
Some but not all requested capabilities granted; operations outside the grant fail authorization (RPH-EXE-004).
Authorization revoked (RuntimeCapabilityRevoked). Revoked bindings cannot be used for new attempts.

stateMachine.note: No dedicated RuntimeBinding transition table; the machine is the authorizationStatus enum (§22) + runtime events (§26.4). Privilege expansion requires a NEW authorization event (§22.1) rather than mutating an existing grant.

transitions.guard: Runtime Authorization Service evaluates capability requests and grants scoped privileges (§33.4).
Revoked bindings cannot be used for new attempts (§22.1).
Privilege expansion requires a NEW authorization event; increments revision but not necessarily PWU semantic version.

runtimeBinding.authorizationRule: A RuntimeBinding MUST be AUTHORIZED before its step executes: the PWU transition PLANNED -> EXECUTING requires 'Runtime bindings authorized' (§8.1), and starting execution with a binding still in REQUESTED is rejected (RPH-EXE-003). Requested capability is not granted capability (§22.1): granted capabilities carry explicit scope, and any operation outside the grant fails authorization -- e.g., a binding that requests file-system AND network but is granted only file-system has its network operations fail authorization (RPH-EXE-004). The Runtime Authorization Service (§33.4) evaluates capability requests, grants scoped privileges, revokes access, and enforces sandbox/tool policies. RuntimeBindings grant no semantic authority and cannot redefine intent or obligations (§2.4); secret access must never be inferred from tool availability, and privilege expansion requires a new authorization event (§22.1).

invariants.statement: Requested capability is not granted capability.
Capability scope must be explicit.
Secret access must never be inferred from tool availability.
Runtime Binding changes increment revision but not necessarily PWU semantic version.
Privilege expansion requires a new authorization event.
Revoked bindings cannot be used for new attempts.
Model output is treated as untrusted external input.

rules.statement: Runtime binding authorization required: starting execution while the runtime binding is in REQUESTED (on an otherwise approved plan) is rejected.
Requested capability is not granted capability: with only file-system granted against a binding requesting file-system and network, network operations fail authorization.

retry.cap: The retry cap is policy-driven by the plan's RetryPolicy. RPH-EXE-008 exercises 'a retry policy with maximum three attempts': when the third retry fails, the controller MUST NOT issue a fourth retry. (The concrete '3' is the test's policy value, not a hardcoded domain constant; the RetryPolicy field shape is not defined in the docs -- see _openItems.)
retry.onExhaustion: On retry exhaustion the controller must not retry again and instead MUST select an alternate control action, one of: change tactic (CHANGE_TACTIC); replan (REPLAN_EXECUTION); escalate (ESCALATE); reject (REJECT); or abandon (ABANDON). 'retry exhaustion' is an enumerated execution failure (§36.2), and each failure class must map to permitted control actions (§36).
retry.attemptTracking: Attempts are tracked as ExecutionAttempt rows with attemptNumber unique per (execution_step_id, attempt_number) (Persistence §10.4); each attempt carries its own idempotencyKey so a retried side-effecting operation does not duplicate its effect (RPH-EXE-007). Retry is emitted as ExecutionStepRetried; escalation/termination as ExecutionEscalated / ExecutionTerminated.

rules.statement: Retry exhaustion triggers alternate control action: given a retry policy with maximum three attempts, when the third retry fails, the controller must not issue a fourth retry; it must select change tactic, replan, escalate, reject, or abandon.
Duplicate external side effect prevented: an execution attempt that creates a source-control commit, when retried with the same idempotency key, produces no second commit.

execVsAssurance: Contract §35.2 (verbatim): `ExecutionStepSucceeded` cannot directly produce `AssuranceState = SATISFIED`. Restated in Contract §16.2: 'Execution-step success does not alter assurance state to SATISFIED.' This is the execution-layer expression of the exec!=assurance separation (INV-5 / the P1 property). Mirrors: Canonical §35.1 ('No execution implies assurance') -- ExecutionState = SUCCEEDED must not automatically imply AssuranceState = SATISFIED; Conformance Property P1 ('Execution never implies assurance') -- executionState = SUCCEEDED must never ALONE cause assuranceState = SATISFIED. Lifecycle consequence (RPH-PWU-005): when a PWU's active plan succeeds, execution state becomes SUCCEEDED and lifecycle becomes EVIDENCE_PENDING, but assurance state is NOT automatically SATISFIED and the PWU lifecycle is NOT SATISFIED; EXECUTING -> SATISFIED without assurance is an illegal transition (§8.3). Rejected assurance still blocks satisfaction even after execution SUCCEEDED (RPH-PWU-007, RPH-E2E-002). Enforced independence is also a projection rule: the UI cannot show unqualified green when execution succeeded but assurance is incomplete (RPH-PRJ-001/002).

restartRecovery.description: In-flight execution is durable and rebuildable: domain events are append-only and authoritative, projections are rebuildable, and a transactional outbox plus command_receipts (idempotency) let the system reconstruct state and resume after a crash WITHOUT duplicating side effects. On restart, active (nonterminal) execution attempts are reconciled before any controller resumes; uncertain external side effects are never blindly retried.
restartRecovery.mechanism: Event-log replay + transactional outbox + command-receipt idempotency keys + per-attempt reconciliation. Recovery ordering (Persistence §34): restore canonical database -> restore artifact storage -> verify event sequence -> replay outbox -> rebuild projections -> reconcile active execution attempts -> resume controllers. Active Execution Recovery (Persistence §35): on restart classify each nonterminal attempt as one of {definitely not started; running with observable external ID; succeeded but result not recorded; failed; completion uncertain}, using the stored external operation ID, invocation timestamp, idempotency key, provider, and reconciliation method; never blindly retry an uncertain side effect; the controller performs reconciliation first.
restartRecovery.idempotencyRule: Commands with the same commandId must not execute twice (§28.2). A duplicate idempotencyKey emits NO new domain events and returns the original result (RPH-PER-002; Contract §35.7; Property P6); command_receipts enforces this (unique idempotency_key: first command -> PROCESSING, then ACCEPTED/REJECTED; duplicate key returns prior result; stale PROCESSING receipts are reconciled). Agent retries must not duplicate commits, external API mutations, baseline promotion, approval decisions, or evidence records (§28.2); side-effecting tool calls use related external idempotency keys, and execution_attempts.idempotency_key is uniquely constrained (uq_execution_idempotency). Duplicate decisions/baselines are specifically prevented (RPH-PER-003, RPH-PER-004). NOTE: the task's citation of 'Persistence §33' for the duplicate-command-replay guarantee is imprecise -- Persistence §33 is 'Data Retention'; the guarantee lives in Persistence §16 (command_receipts), Contract §35.7, RPH-PER-002, and Property P6 (see _openItems).

rules.statement: Restart after command persistence: if events are committed but the projection update is interrupted, after the service restarts the projection is eventually updated from the event/outbox stream.

Restart during model invocation: if an execution attempt was started and completion is uncertain, on restart the attempt enters a reconciliation state and the system must not blindly repeat the side effect.
Restart during human wait: if a PWU is waiting for human approval and the extension restarts, the pending decision remains available and no duplicate review request is created.

Restart after validator result but before assessment disposition: if a valid raw validator result is persisted and the Assurance Service restarts before authoritative disposition, processing resumes idempotently.
Outbox atomicity: on a successful aggregate mutation, domain events and outbox rows are committed atomically; a transaction rollback leaves neither persisted.
Aggregate replay equivalence: reconstructing an aggregate from its event stream yields state matching the materialized current state.

Restart during architecture execution: if the extension/runtime restarts before completion status is known, attempt state is reconciled, duplicate generation/side effects are avoided, execution resumes or is retried per policy, and event history remains coherent.

conformanceTests.name: One active plan per PWU
conformanceTests.given: a PWU with an active plan
conformanceTests.when: a second plan is activated without superseding the first
conformanceTests.then: the command is rejected

conformanceTests.name: Superseded plan cannot start step
conformanceTests.given: an execution plan in SUPERSEDED
conformanceTests.then: the command is rejected

conformanceTests.name: Runtime binding authorization required
conformanceTests.given: an approved execution plan and a runtime binding in REQUESTED
conformanceTests.then: the command is rejected

conformanceTests.name: Requested capability is not granted capability
conformanceTests.given: a runtime binding requesting file-system and network access; only file-system access is granted
conformanceTests.then: network operations fail authorization

conformanceTests.name: Step precondition enforcement
conformanceTests.given: an execution step whose required input artifact is absent
conformanceTests.then: the step remains not ready and no model/tool invocation occurs

conformanceTests.name: Step success requires explicit result
conformanceTests.given: a running step
conformanceTests.when: completion is requested without output or explicit no-output result
conformanceTests.then: completion is rejected

conformanceTests.name: Duplicate external side effect prevented
conformanceTests.given: an execution attempt that creates a source-control commit
conformanceTests.when: the command is retried with the same idempotency key
conformanceTests.then: no second commit occurs

conformanceTests.name: Retry exhaustion triggers alternate control action
conformanceTests.given: a retry policy with maximum three attempts
conformanceTests.then: the controller must not issue a fourth retry; it must select change tactic, replan, escalate, reject, or abandon

conformanceTests.name: Model output treated as untrusted
conformanceTests.given: a model returns a malformed structured result
conformanceTests.then: raw output is retained for diagnostics; boundary validation fails; authoritative objects are not created; retry or alternate strategy may be selected

conformanceTests.name: Execution success does not satisfy PWU
conformanceTests.given: a PWU in EXECUTING
conformanceTests.then: execution state becomes SUCCEEDED; lifecycle becomes EVIDENCE_PENDING; assurance state is not automatically SATISFIED; PWU lifecycle is not SATISFIED

conformanceTests.name: Rejected assurance prevents satisfaction
conformanceTests.given: execution state SUCCEEDED and a required assurance assessment REJECTED
conformanceTests.then: the PWU cannot transition to SATISFIED

conformanceTests.name: Baselined PWU cannot resume execution in place
conformanceTests.given: a PWU in BASELINED
conformanceTests.when: new execution is requested against the same semantic version
conformanceTests.then: the command is rejected; a successor revision or successor PWU is required

conformanceTests.name: Architecture execution succeeds but assurance fails
conformanceTests.given: architecture generation succeeds but lacks enforceable tenant isolation
conformanceTests.then: execution state is SUCCEEDED; assurance state is REJECTED; the PWU is not satisfied; a blocking observation is created; baseline promotion is unavailable; controller recommends reshape or replan

conformanceTests.name: Restart during architecture execution
conformanceTests.given: architecture generation begins
conformanceTests.when: the extension or runtime restarts before completion status is known
conformanceTests.then: attempt state is reconciled; duplicate generation/side effects avoided; execution resumes or is retried per policy; event history remains coherent

conformanceTests.name: Duplicate command returns prior result
conformanceTests.given: a previously accepted command with an idempotency key
conformanceTests.when: the same key is submitted again
conformanceTests.then: no new domain events are emitted; the original result is returned

conformanceTests.name: Duplicate decision prevented
conformanceTests.given: an approval request
conformanceTests.then: it cannot create two effective decisions

conformanceTests.name: Duplicate baseline prevented
conformanceTests.given: a baseline-promotion command
conformanceTests.then: it cannot create two authoritative baselines

conformanceTests.name: Aggregate replay equivalence
conformanceTests.given: an event stream for an Intent or PWU
conformanceTests.then: its state matches the materialized current state

conformanceTests.name: Outbox atomicity
conformanceTests.given: a successful aggregate mutation
conformanceTests.when: events and outbox rows are written
conformanceTests.then: they are committed atomically; a transaction rollback leaves neither persisted

conformanceTests.name: Restart after command persistence
conformanceTests.given: events are committed but projection update is interrupted
conformanceTests.then: the projection is eventually updated from the event/outbox stream

conformanceTests.name: Restart during model invocation
conformanceTests.given: an execution attempt was started and the external operation's completion is uncertain
conformanceTests.then: the attempt enters a reconciliation state; the system must not blindly repeat the side effect

conformanceTests.name: Restart during human wait
conformanceTests.given: a PWU waiting for human approval
conformanceTests.then: the pending decision remains available and no duplicate review request is created

conformanceTests.name: Restart after validator result but before assessment disposition
conformanceTests.given: a valid raw validator result persisted
conformanceTests.when: the Assurance Service restarts before authoritative disposition
conformanceTests.then: processing resumes idempotently

conformanceTests.name: Expected event replay
conformanceTests.given: the reference undertaking expected-events.jsonl
conformanceTests.then: produces approved Intent Baseline; satisfied Product Behavior PWU; authoritative Architecture Baseline; expected open residual condition

conformanceTests.name: Execution never implies assurance
conformanceTests.given: any generated legal command sequence
conformanceTests.then: must never ALONE cause assuranceState = SATISFIED

conformanceTests.name: Idempotent commands
conformanceTests.given: any command with an idempotency key
conformanceTests.when: the same idempotency key is executed multiple times
conformanceTests.then: produces the same business result and no additional domain events

conformanceTests.name: Presentation independence
conformanceTests.given: arbitrary layout/canvas changes
conformanceTests.then: semantic object versions and assurance states remain unchanged

enumsIntroduced.name: ExecutionPlanStatus
enumsIntroduced.note: Canonical §20.1 = Contract §15 (identical spellings). One ACTIVE per PWU enforced in persistence.

enumsIntroduced.name: ExecutionStepType
enumsIntroduced.note: §21. ASSURANCE_INVOCATION may trigger assurance but does not by itself set AssuranceState=SATISFIED (§35.2).

enumsIntroduced.name: ExecutionStepState
enumsIntroduced.note: §21. Event names (§26.4) do not 1:1 match: Started<->RUNNING, Ready<->READY; no dedicated events for QUEUED/NOT_READY/SUPERSEDED (see _openItems).

enumsIntroduced.name: RuntimeBindingAuthorizationStatus
enumsIntroduced.note: §22. AUTHORIZED required before a step executes.

enumsIntroduced.name: ControlAction
enumsIntroduced.note: §37 Controller Decision Contract. On retry exhaustion the permitted subset is {CHANGE_TACTIC, REPLAN_EXECUTION, ESCALATE, REJECT, ABANDON} (RPH-EXE-008). Every control action records triggering condition, evidence/observations considered, authorizing policy, actor, affected objects, expected outcome.

enumsIntroduced.name: ExecutionState (result)
enumsIntroduced.note: Referenced (resultingExecutionState:'SUCCEEDED'; PWU matrix 'Execution state is SUCCEEDED'; failure taxonomy) but NOT enumerated as a single named type in the docs -- SUCCEEDED and FAILED are the two observed values; a full enumeration is not provided (see _openItems).

enumsIntroduced.name: ExecutionFailureClass (§36.2)
enumsIntroduced.note: Prose taxonomy, not a code enum; each class must map to permitted control actions (§36).

enumsIntroduced.name: DualRunComparisonClassification
enumsIntroduced.note: Shadow/dual-run comparison classification (Persistence §29). Relevant to dual-run migration validation, not to core execution runtime.

_openItems.detail: Canonical §20.1 references steps and transitions by id (stepIds: string[], transitionIds: string[]); Contract §15 embeds them inline (steps: ExecutionStep[], transitions: ExecutionTransition[]). Persistence normalizes into separate execution_steps / execution_transitions tables (FK execution_plan_id), matching the Canonical id-reference model. The embedded Contract shape is the wire/command shape (ProposeExecutionPlanPayload); the id-reference shape is the stored/aggregate shape.

The task cites '§35.2' as the verbatim boundary. In the Canonical Domain Model doc this property is §35.1 ('No execution implies assurance'). The verbatim '`ExecutionStepSucceeded` cannot directly produce AssuranceState = SATISFIED' is CONTRACT PACKAGE §35.2 (and Contract §16.2). Both are recorded; the operative verbatim string is Contract §35.2.

The task references 'Property P6/P8 (execution / idempotency)'. In the Conformance Spec (§25) the execution-independence property is P1 ('Execution never implies assurance'); P6 is 'Idempotent commands'; P8 is 'Presentation independence' (NOT execution). Actual spellings recorded in conformanceTests. So: execution-vs-assurance = P1, idempotency = P6, presentation = P8.

The task cites Persistence §33 for 'duplicate command replay does not create duplicate ...'. Persistence §33 is actually 'Data Retention'. The idempotency/duplicate-command guarantee lives in Persistence §16 (command_receipts: 'duplicate key returns prior result'), Contract §35.7 ('A duplicate idempotency key produces no additional domain event'), Conformance RPH-PER-002/003/004, and Property P6.

RetryPolicy, TacticalChangePolicy, EscalationPolicy, TerminationPolicy are declared as ExecutionPlan field types but their internal shapes (e.g., a maxAttempts field) are never defined in any doc. RPH-EXE-008's 'maximum three attempts' is the test fixture's policy value, not a domain constant. Any implementation must define these policy shapes; the retry cap must be read from RetryPolicy, not hardcoded.

The test title says 'maximum three attempts' but the body says 'the third retry fails' then 'must not issue a fourth retry', which conflates attempts with retries (3 attempts vs 3 retries = 4 attempts). The invariant to implement is unambiguous (no retry beyond the cap -> alternate control action), but the exact numeric mapping (is the cap 3 total attempts or 3 retries?) is ambiguous in the doc.

_openItems.item: stepState enum vs execution events mismatch
_openItems.detail: ExecutionStepState includes NOT_READY, QUEUED, RUNNING, SUPERSEDED, but §26.4 execution events are ExecutionStepReady/Started/Waiting/Succeeded/Failed/Retried/Skipped/Cancelled -- 'Started' implies RUNNING and there are no dedicated events for QUEUED, NOT_READY, or step-level SUPERSEDED. The state<->event name reconciliation is left implicit.

_openItems.item: ExecutionState result enum not fully enumerated
_openItems.detail: Payloads use resultingExecutionState:'SUCCEEDED' and the PWU matrix references 'Execution state is SUCCEEDED', but a complete ExecutionState enum (beyond SUCCEEDED and the FAILED implied by failure taxonomy) is not defined in one authoritative place.

_openItems.item: Restart reconciliation_state has no enumerated values
_openItems.detail: Persistence §35 gives a PROSE classification of nonterminal attempts on restart (definitely not started / running with observable external ID / succeeded but result not recorded / failed / completion uncertain), and execution_attempts.reconciliation_state is a free-text column. RPH-PER-012 requires 'a reconciliation state' without specifying enumerated values. An implementation must define this enum.

_openItems.item: Execution Attempt not in the Canonical interface
_openItems.detail: Execution Attempt is owned by the Execution Aggregate (§3.3) and is where retries/side-effects/idempotency-keys live (Persistence §10.4), but there is no ExecutionAttempt interface in Canonical §20-22; its fields are only defined by the persistence table. Recorded under executionStep.executionAttempt.
